title: helianthemum pilosum subsp. lineare
intro: Helianthemum pilosum subsp. lineare, also known as rockrose or cliff rockrose, is a flowering plant species within the family Cistaceae, native to the Iberian Peninsula and North Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity, making it a popular ingredient in traditional herbal remedies. Helianthemum pilosum subsp. lineare can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal preparations such as creams and ointments to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
